```{r DIET and WEIGHT}
  labs(x = "Settlement Diet", y = "Weight (g)")
```
A Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test was performed to compare the effect of the various settlement diet treatments on star weight (g) post-settlement. The Kruskal-Wallis test and anova revealed that there was not a statistically significant difference in weight between at least two groups (KW Chi2(3) = 3.462, p = 0.33)). A non-parametric Dunn's Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of weight was no statistically significant difference between any particular settlement diet groups.



```{r DIET and LENGTH}
##Did the post-settlement diet impact the growth of settled stars?

#LENGTH df, exact same workflow as above
Diet_length <- fitness %>% 
  drop_na(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  group_by(Settlement_diet) %>% 
   mutate(mean_length = mean(Length_mm),
         se = sd(Length_mm)/sqrt(length((Length_mm)))) 

Diet_length_no_outliers <- fitness %>% 
  drop_na(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  filter(Flip_time_seconds < 500) %>%
  group_by(Settlement_diet) %>% 
  mutate(mean_length = mean(Length_mm),
         se = sd(Length_mm)/sqrt(length((Length_mm)))) 

#visually check differences in mean
ggplot(Diet_length, aes(x = Settlement_diet, y = mean_length, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.1, colour = "blue", 
                aes(ymin = mean_length - se, ymax = mean_length + se)) +
  labs(x = "Settlement Diet", y = "Mean Length (mm)") +
  theme_classic2()

#descriptive stats
by(Diet_length$Length_mm, Diet_length$Settlement_diet, stat.desc)

#checking variances
leveneTest(Diet_length$Length_mm, Diet_length$Settlement_diet, center = median)
##appropriate! p = 0.33, fulfills assumptions

#checking normality
ggplot(Diet_length, aes(sample = Length_mm)) +
     geom_qq() +
     geom_qq_line() +
     facet_wrap(~Settlement_diet, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
  theme_few()
#deviation in 1/4 of groups (worms and diatoms seems to be bimodal is dist)

#shapiro.test between and within groups
shapiro.test(Diet_length$Length_mm) #overall weight, p = 0.5018

test <- Diet_length %>% dplyr::select(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  filter(Settlement_diet == "Juv. stars")
shapiro.test(test$Length_mm) #juv stars p = 0.9154, normal

test <- Diet_length %>% dplyr::select(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  filter(Settlement_diet == "Kitchen sink")
shapiro.test(test$Length_mm) #kitchen sink, p = 0.3949, normal

test <- Diet_length %>% dplyr::select(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  filter(Settlement_diet == "MLML Urchins")
shapiro.test(test$Length_mm) #MLML urchins, p = 0.9354, normal

test <- Diet_length %>% dplyr::select(Settlement_diet, Length_mm) %>%
  filter(Settlement_diet == "Worms & Diatoms")
shapiro.test(test$Length_mm) #worms and diatoms, p = 0.3751
##all good here, proceeding with regular and robust anova tests

par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) #reset plot params

#anova, as a result of only kitchen sink showing deviation from normality 
Diet_length_aov <- aov(Length_mm ~ Settlement_diet, data = Diet_length)
summary(Diet_length_aov) #p = 0.366
plot(Diet_length_aov)

#robust anova
t1way(Length_mm ~ Settlement_diet, data = Diet_length) #default 20%
##p = 0.07849
t1way(Length_mm ~ Settlement_diet, data = Diet_length, tr = .1) #trim only 10%
##p = 0.0998
med1way(Length_mm ~ Settlement_diet, data = Diet_length)
##p = 0.128

#post-hoc tests
Diet_length_posthoc <- Diet_length_aov %>% tukey_hsd() #tukey's test
Diet_length_posthoc #view results

#work flow for publication-ready plot
Diet_length_posthoc <- Diet_length_posthoc %>% arrange(p.adj) #arrange
Diet_length_posthoc <- add_xy_position(test = Diet_length_posthoc,
  data = Diet_length %>% as_tibble(.name_repair = "unique"),  #unique column names
  formula = Length_mm ~ Density, fun = "mean", x = "Settlement_diet")

#factor levels in the order used in the plot
x_levels <- levels(factor(Diet_length$Settlement_diet))

#vector to convert group names to x-axis positions
x_map <- setNames(seq_along(x_levels), x_levels)

#map to assign numeric values to xmin and xmax
Diet_length_posthoc <- Diet_length_posthoc %>%
  mutate(xmin = x_map[group1], xmax = x_map[group2])

#adjusting y.position to fit the signf brackets
Diet_length_posthoc <- Diet_length_posthoc %>% mutate(y.position = y.position + 40)
Diet_length_posthoc$y.position[2] <- 95 #specific y position of 2nd signf bracket

#now that i know the workflow for this, its a walk in the park
ggboxplot(Diet_length, x = "Settlement_diet", y = "Length_mm", 
          color = "Settlement_diet", add = "jitter", shape = "Settlement_diet") + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(Diet_length_posthoc, label = "p.adj.signif", hide.ns = TRUE) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 100, label.x = "Kitchen sink") +
  labs(x = "Settlement Diet", y = "Length (mm)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 105))  #extend y-axis to fit signf brackets

report(Diet_length_aov)
```
A one-way ANOVA was performed to compare the effect of settlement diet treatment on Length (mm).A one-way ANOVA revealed that there was no statistically significant difference in Length between at least two groups (F(3, 83) = 1.55, p = 0.208)). Tukey’s HSD Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of Length was not significantly different between any of the settlement diet groups.




## Linear regression(s)
Asking: how might we be able to apply analysis or statistics to variables in flip time? Could this help us determine a particular trend in linearity between variables? 
Used base r "stats" package to run lm()

```{r LENGTH and FLIP TIME}
##Do longer stars flip over slower? 

Size_flip <- fitness %>% 
  drop_na(Length_mm, Weight_g, Flip_time, Flip_time_seconds)

Size_flip_no_outliers <- fitness %>% 
  drop_na(Length_mm, Weight_g, Flip_time, Flip_time_seconds) %>%
  filter(Flip_time_seconds < 1000) #13/88 observations with flip times above 500 seconds

#test normality visually (qq-plot/hist)
hist(Size_flip$Length_mm) #fairly normal
hist(Size_flip$Flip_time_seconds) #right skew, non-normal
qqnorm(Size_flip$Length_mm) #length is normal
qqnorm(Size_flip$Flip_time_seconds) #flip time is NOT normal

#also check normality numerically with shapiro (just simply for my learning)
shapiro.test(Size_flip$Length_mm) #p=0.44, no deviation from normality
shapiro.test(Size_flip$Flip_time_seconds) #p=1.411e-12, HIGH deviation from normality

#running a linear regression model
Flip_length_lm <- lm(formula = Flip_time_seconds ~ Length_mm, data = Size_flip)
Flip_length_lm
summary(Flip_length_lm)
sqrt(0.03947) #pearson coefficient is = 0.1986
AIC(Flip_length_lm) #AIC = 1277.18

#flip_time ~ length model diagnostics
model.diag.metrics <- augment(Flip_length_lm)
head(model.diag.metrics)

#plotted residuals error (red) between observed values and fitted regression line
ggplot(model.diag.metrics, aes(Length_mm, Flip_time_seconds)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm, se = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Length_mm, yend = .fitted), color = "red", size = 0.3) +
  labs(x = "Length (mm)", y = "Flip Time (seconds)") +
  theme_classic2()
```
Simple linear regression was used to test if Length (mm) significantly predicted P. helianothoides flip time from the February fitness test. The fitted regression model was: [Flip time = 549 x -5.424*(Length)]. The overall regression was statistically insignificant (R2 = 0.039, F(1, 86) = 3.534, p = 0.0635).It was found that length is not an accurate predictor of flip time (β = -5.242, p = 0.0635).


```{r WEIGHT and FLIP TIME }
##Do small stars maneuver or flip faster?

#using same working df's from above question!

#test normality visually (qq-plot/hist)
hist(Size_flip$Weight_g) #right skew
qqnorm(Size_flip$Weight_g) #non-normal
qqline(Size_flip$Weight_g) #non-normal

#confirm with shapiro (we already know Flip time is not normal, given above)
shapiro.test(Size_flip$Weight_g) #p=0.00007 weight is NOT normal... apparently

#running a linear regression model
Flip_weight_lm <- lm(formula = Flip_time_seconds ~ Weight_g, data = Size_flip)
summary(Flip_weight_lm) #p = 0.1189
sqrt(0.02804) #pearson coefficient is = 0.1674515

#flip_time ~ length model diagnostics
model.diag.metrics <- augment(Flip_weight_lm)
head(model.diag.metrics)

#plotted residuals error (red) between observed values and fitted regression line
ggplot(model.diag.metrics, aes(Weight_g, Flip_time_seconds)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm, se = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Weight_g, yend = .fitted), color = "red", size = 0.3) +
  labs(x = "Weight (g)", y = "Flip Time (seconds)") +
  theme_classic2()
```
Weight of a sea-star can account for 2.8% (R2 = 0.028) of the variation in sea-star flip time (given outliers are included in the analysis). This model, similar to the first length model, DOES NOT accurately predict the variation in sea-star flip time given the variation in star length (F=2.481, p=0.1189).


```{r WEIGHT and FLIP TIME no outliers}
#linear regression with NON outlier data

#normality tests with NON outlier data (qq-plot/hist)
hist(Size_flip_no_outliers$Weight_g) #still right skew
qqnorm(Size_flip_no_outliers$Weight_g) 
qqline(Size_flip_no_outliers$Weight_g) #better fit, but still non-normal

#shapiro test
shapiro.test(Size_flip_no_outliers$Weight_g) #still non-normal without outliers (p = 0.00027)

#regression with NO OUTLIER data
Flip_weight_lm_2 <- lm(formula = Flip_time_seconds ~ Weight_g, data = Size_flip_no_outliers)
summary(Flip_weight_lm_2) #signf, (p = 0.002)
sqrt(0.05619) #pearson coefficient = 0.237
```
Weight of a sea-star can account for 5.6% (R2 = 0.05619) of the variation in sea-star flip time (given outliers are NOT included in the analysis). This model, similar to the first CAN accurately predict the variation in sea-star flip time given the variation in star length (F = 4.822, p = 0.03).



```{r ARM COUNT and FLIP TIME}
##Does the number of arms a star have correlate to the speed of flip time?

#normality check
hist(Size_flip$Arm_count) #bi-modal dist
qqnorm(Size_flip$Arm_count) #VERY bi-modal
qqline(Size_flip$Arm_count)

#normality test
shapiro.test(Size_flip$Arm_count) #non-normal, p = 1.3e-5

#regression w/ flip time outliers
Flip_arms_1 <- lm(formula = Flip_time_seconds ~ Arm_count, data = Size_flip)
summary(Flip_arms_1)
sqrt(0.04663) #pearson coef = 0.2159
```
Results...


```{r ARM COUNT and FLIP TIME no outliers}
#regression with NON outlier data

#normality check
hist(Size_flip_no_outliers$Arm_count) 
qqnorm(Size_flip_no_outliers$Arm_count) #still very bi-modal
qqline(Size_flip_no_outliers$Arm_count)

#normality test
shapiro.test(Size_flip_no_outliers$Arm_count) #non-normal, p = 1.924e-5

#regression w/out flip time outliers
Flip_arms_2 <- lm(formula = Flip_time_seconds ~ Arm_count, data = Size_flip_no_outliers)
summary(Flip_arms_2)

```
Results...




## Multiple regression analyses
```{r checking homo/heteroscedasticity}
#using same working df's as above, testing for homoscedasticity in predictor variables
Multi_model_test <- lm(Flip_time_seconds ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Arm_count + Eating_score + Wellness_score, data = Size_flip) #choosing these 5 predictor variables for the model, checking assumptions below

ncvTest(Multi_model_test) 
##data is not fulfilling assumptions, showing heteroscedasticity (p= 5.12e-8)

#testing predictors individually
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm) #p=0.16, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Weight_g) #p=0.62, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Arm_count) #p= 0.0016, NOT GOOD
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Eating_score) #p=7.51e-6, NOT GOOD 
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Wellness_score) #p=0.08, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g) #p=0.11, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Arm_count) #p=0.004, NOT GOOD
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Wellness_score) #p=0.04, NOT GOOD
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Eating_score) #p=1.49e-5, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Eating_score + Arm_count) #p=1.78e-6, NOT GOOD
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Weight_g + Eating_score + Wellness_score) #p=bad
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Length_mm + Wellness_score) #p=0.06, good
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Weight_g + Wellness_score) #p=0.18, better than length
ncvTest(Multi_model_test, ~ Weight_g + Wellness_score + Eating_score) #p=bad
```
Arm_count AND Eating score are heteroscedastic, therefore will not be included in the model, however wellness score is heteroscedastic when with weight/length



```{r multiple regression model with FLIP TIME by WEIGHT and WELLNESS SCORE}
#model without Arm_count, Eating_score, and without Length_mm (given result of heteroscedasticity pairing above)
Multi_model_1 <- lm(Flip_time_seconds ~ Weight_g + Wellness_score, data = Size_flip)
summary(Multi_model_1)
```
This model can explain 3.5% of the variation in Flip time (R2 = 0.035), as opposed to 3.9% in just the model fit with Length.
    Non significant model results (F = 1.543, p = 0.2196)
    No particular parameter is making a significant contribution to the model given Pr(>|t|) all are >0.05


```{r model diagnostics}
#reporting standardized ß measurements
#lm.beta(Multi_model_1) #code no longer working, ignore for now
##Given standardized units, as weight increases or changes, flip time will decrease and as Wellness_sore increases, flip time will also increase by units according to the standardized ß scores (Weight_gß = -0.17, Wellness_scoreß = 0.08). 

#confidence intervals of regression model
confint(Multi_model_1)

#Residuals for multi-model 1 (model with outliers)
Size_flip$Residuals <- resid(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Standard_residuals <- rstandard(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Studentized_residuals <- rstudent(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Cooks_distance <- cooks.distance(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Df_beta <- dfbeta(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Df_fit <- dffits(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Leverage <- hatvalues(Multi_model_1)
Size_flip$Covariance_ratios <- covratio(Multi_model_1)

#large residuals search; every case when residual is less than -2 or greater than 
Size_flip$Large_residuals <- Size_flip$Standard_residuals > 2 | 
  Size_flip$Standard_residuals < -2 
sum(Size_flip$Large_residuals) #6 cases with a large residual

#identifying large residuals
Size_flip[Size_flip$Large_residuals, c("Weight_g", "Wellness_score", "Standard_residuals")] 

#looking at other values where large residuals is TRUE
Size_flip[Size_flip$Large_residuals, c("Cooks_distance", "Leverage", "Covariance_ratios")] 
```
    none of the cooks distance numbers are greater than one, so these cases are not having an undue influence on the model. 
    No cases have a higher average leverage. 
    NONE of the cases here have CVR values within the bounds (1.06-0.94). There is undue influence from all of these cases according to the CVR


```{r model diagnostics}
#Durban Watson test of independence
dwt(Multi_model_1)
##D-W stat = 1.32, semi-close to 2, and a p = 0.008, so we cannot assume independence ???

#plotting studentized residuals, fitted values, and theoretical values of the model
Size_flip$Fitted <- Multi_model_1$fitted.values

#histogram with dnorm line
histogram.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip, aes(Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), colour = "black", fill = "white") +
  labs(x = "Studentized Residuals", y = "Density") +
  theme_clean()
histogram.residuals <- histogram.residuals + stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(
  mean = mean(Size_flip$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd = sd(Size_flip$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  colour = "red", size = 0.5) +
  theme_clean()

#Q-Q plot
qqplot.residuals <- ggplot() +
  geom_qq(data = Size_flip, aes(sample = Studentized_residuals)) + 
  labs(x = "Theoretical Values", y = "Observed Values") +
  theme_clean()

#scatter
scatter.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip, aes(Fitted, Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm, colour = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Fitted Values", y = "Stundentized Residual") +
  theme_clean()

#patchwork graphs for viewing
histogram.residuals + qqplot.residuals + scatter.residuals
```
model is fairly (kinda?) accurate for our population, but this model cannot be generalized to a greater population in better determining responses of sea-stars to environmental stimuli (ie. being pushed upside down, then righting oneself)


```{r multiple regression model with FLIP TIME by WEIGHT and WELLNESS SCORE no outliers}
#Multi model without flip time outliers 
Multi_model_1_no <- lm(Flip_time_seconds ~ Weight_g + Wellness_score, data = Size_flip_no_outliers)
summary(Multi_model_1_no)
```
This model explains 12.8% in the variation (R2 = 0.165) of Flip time as opposed to 11.2% (R2 = 0.112) variation from just length without flip time outliers simple model. 
There is a significant relationship in this model (p = 0.007)
Adjusted R2 is different by xxx, so cross validity of the model is... okay

```{r model diagnostics}
#standardized beta scores
#lm.beta(Multi_model_1_no) 
##Given standardized units, as weight AND wellness score increase or change, flip time will decrease according to the standardized ß scores (Weight_gß = -0.32, Wellness_scoreß = 0.11). 

#confidence intervals of regression model
confint(Multi_model_1_no)

#Residuals for multi-model 1 (model with outliers)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Residuals <- resid(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals <- rstandard(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals <- rstudent(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Cooks_distance <- cooks.distance(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Df_beta <- dfbeta(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Df_fit <- dffits(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Leverage <- hatvalues(Multi_model_1_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Covariance_ratios <- covratio(Multi_model_1_no)

#large residuals search  
Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals <- Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals > 2 |
  Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals < -2  
sum(Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals) #5 cases with a large residual vs. 6 WITH outliers

#identifying large residuals
Size_flip_no_outliers[Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Weight_g", "Wellness_score", "Standard_residuals")]
Size_flip_no_outliers[Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Cooks_distance", "Leverage", "Covariance_ratios")]
```
    none of the cooks distance numbers are greater than one, so these cases are not having an undue influence on the model. 
    instance 2 has a notable larger cooks distance than the others
    No cases have a higher average leverage. instance 2 has another high leverage score
    NONE of the cases here have CVR values within the bounds (1.06-0.94). 
    There is undue influence from all of these cases according to the CVR, excpet for case 2, which has a CVR = 0.95. 

```{r model diagnostics}
#Durban Watson test of independence
dwt(Multi_model_1)
##D-W stat = 1.32, semi-close to 2, and a p = 0.006, so we cannot assume independence ???

#plotting studentized residuals, fitted values, and theoretical values of the model
Size_flip_no_outliers$Fitted <- Multi_model_1_no$fitted.values

#histogram with dnorm line
histogram.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), colour = "black", fill = "white") +
  labs(x = "Studentized Residuals", y = "Density") +
  theme_clean()
histogram.residuals + stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(
  mean = mean(Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd = sd(Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  colour = "red", size = 0.5) +
  theme_clean()

#Q-Q plot
qqplot.residuals <- ggplot() +
  geom_qq(data = Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(sample = Studentized_residuals)) + 
  labs(x = "Theoretical Values", y = "Observed Values") +
  theme_clean()

#scatter
scatter.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(Fitted, Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm, colour = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Fitted Values", y = "Stundentized Residual") +
  theme_clean()

#patchwork graphs for viewing
histogram.residuals + qqplot.residuals + scatter.residuals
```
Again, model is fairly (kinda?) accurate for our population, but this model still cannot be generalized to a greater population (even without extreme cases)



```{r MLR with FLIP TIME by LENGTH and WELLNESS SCORE}
Multi_model_2 <- lm(Flip_time_seconds ~ Length_mm + Wellness_score, data = Size_flip)
summary(Multi_model_2)
```
This model can explain 4.7% in the variation (R2 = 0.0477) of Flip time as opposed to 3.5% of the variation in the previous mutli model (flip time ~ weight + wellness score) with outliers. 
There is NO significant relationship in this model (p = 0.1252)
Adjusted R2 is different by 0.02241, so cross validity of the model is okay, but still not strong


```{r model diagnostics}
#standardized beta scores
#lm.beta(Multi_model_2) 
##Given standardized units, length and wellness score have opposite effects on flip time, as length increases flip time decreases (Length_mmß = -0.21) and as wellness score increases (Wellness_scoreß = 0.09), flip time increases? TBH this is a super weird result and I don't truly trust the validity of this model 

#confidence intervals of regression model
confint(Multi_model_2)
##

#Residuals for multi-model 2 (model without outliers)
Size_flip$Residuals <- resid(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Standard_residuals <- rstandard(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Studentized_residuals <- rstudent(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Cooks_distance <- cooks.distance(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Df_beta <- dfbeta(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Df_fit <- dffits(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Leverage <- hatvalues(Multi_model_2)
Size_flip$Covariance_ratios <- covratio(Multi_model_2)

#save new data
write.table(Size_flip, "Multi model 2 with diagnostics.dat", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)

#large residuals search  
Size_flip$Large_residuals <- 
  Size_flip$Standard_residuals > 2 |
  Size_flip$Standard_residuals < -2 # 
sum(Size_flip$Large_residuals) #6 cases with a large residual 

#identifying large residuals
Size_flip[Size_flip$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Weight_g", "Wellness_score", "Standard_residuals")]
Size_flip[Size_flip$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Cooks_distance", "Leverage", "Covariance_ratios")]
```
none of the cooks distance numbers are greater than one, so these cases are not having an undue influence on the model. instance 23 has a notably higher cooks distance than the others
No cases have a higher average leverage
NONE of the cases have a CVR value within the bounds (1.06-0.94). 
There is undue influence from 6 of these cases according to the CVR 


```{r model diagnostics}
#Durban Watson test of independence
dwt(Multi_model_2)
##D-W stat = 1.29, semi-close to 2, and a p = 0.004, same value as Multi_model_1

#plotting studentized residuals, fitted values, and theoretical values of the model
Size_flip$Fitted <- Multi_model_2$fitted.values

#histogram with dnorm line
histogram.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip, aes(Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), colour = "black", fill = "white") +
  labs(x = "Studentized Residuals", y = "Density") +
  theme_clean()
histogram.residuals <- histogram.residuals + stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(
  mean = mean(Size_flip$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd = sd(Size_flip$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  colour = "red", size = 0.5) +
  theme_clean()

#Q-Q plot
qqplot.residuals <- ggplot() +
  geom_qq(data = Size_flip, aes(sample = Studentized_residuals)) + 
  labs(x = "Theoretical Values", y = "Observed Values") +
  theme_clean()

#scatter
scatter.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip, aes(Fitted, Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm, colour = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Fitted Values", y = "Stundentized Residual") +
  theme_clean()

#patchwork graphs for viewing
histogram.residuals + qqplot.residuals + scatter.residuals
```
Same as above results



```{r MRM with FLIP TIME by LENGTH and WELLNESS SCORE no outliers}
Multi_model_2_no <- lm(Flip_time_seconds ~ Length_mm + Wellness_score, data = Size_flip_no_outliers)
summary(Multi_model_2_no)
```
This model can explain 12.5% in the variation (R2 = 0.1254) of Flip time as opposed to the 12.8% of the variation in the previous multi model (flip time ~ weight + wellness score) run without outliers. 
There is NO significant relationship in this model (p = 0.1252)
Adjusted R2 is different by 0.02241, so cross validity of the model is okay, but still not strong


```{r model diagnostics}
#standardized beta scores
#lm.beta(Multi_model_2_no) 
##Given standardized units, length and wellness score have both negative ß scores (Length_mmß = -0.322) and as wellness score increases (Wellness_scoreß = -0.116)

#confidence intervals of regression model
confint(Multi_model_2_no)

#Residuals for multi-model 2 (model without outliers)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Residuals <- resid(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals <- rstandard(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals <- rstudent(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Cooks_distance <- cooks.distance(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Df_beta <- dfbeta(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Df_fit <- dffits(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Leverage <- hatvalues(Multi_model_2_no)
Size_flip_no_outliers$Covariance_ratios <- covratio(Multi_model_2_no)

#large residuals search  
Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals <- Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals > 2 |
  Size_flip_no_outliers$Standard_residuals < -2  
sum(Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals) #4 cases with a large residual 

#identifying large residuals
Size_flip_no_outliers[Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Weight_g", "Wellness_score", "Standard_residuals")]
Size_flip_no_outliers[Size_flip_no_outliers$Large_residuals, 
                      c("Cooks_distance", "Leverage", "Covariance_ratios")]
```
none of the cooks distance numbers are greater than one, so these cases are not having an undue influence on the model. instance 2 and 42 have notably higher ratios than the other values
No cases have a higher average leverage
NONE of the cases have a CVR value within the bounds (1.06-0.94) but instance 2 and 42 have higher values (approaching 0.95). There is undue influence from 2/4 of these cases according to the CVR 


```{r model diagnostics}
#Durban Watson test of independence
dwt(Multi_model_2_no)
##D-W stat = 1.59, much closer to 2 than any of the other models, and a p = 0.064, so independence is not assumed and likely violated

#still plotting studentized residuals, fitted values, and theoretical values of the model to assess fit of residuals 
Size_flip_no_outliers$Fitted <- Multi_model_2_no$fitted.values

#histogram with dnorm line
histogram.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), colour = "black", fill = "white") +
  labs(x = "Studentized Residuals", y = "Density") +
  theme_clean()
histogram.residuals <- histogram.residuals + stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(
  mean = mean(Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd = sd(Size_flip_no_outliers$Studentized_residuals, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  colour = "red", size = 0.5) +
  theme_clean()

#Q-Q plot
qqplot.residuals <- ggplot() +
  geom_qq(data = Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(sample = Studentized_residuals)) + 
  labs(x = "Theoretical Values", y = "Observed Values") +
  theme_clean()

#scatter
scatter.residuals <- ggplot(Size_flip_no_outliers, aes(Fitted, Studentized_residuals)) +
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm, colour = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Fitted Values", y = "Stundentized Residual") +
  theme_clean()

#patchwork graphs for viewing
histogram.residuals + qqplot.residuals + scatter.residuals
```


## Plotting -- settlement data
```{r cumulative SETTLEMENT overtime}
#fixing labeling for plots
settlement_raw_long$Densities <- factor(
  settlement_raw_long$Density, 
  levels = c("1larva/ml", "2larva/ml", "5then2larva/ml", "Bucket"),
  labels = c("1 larvae/mL", "2 larvae/mL", "5 larvae/mL", "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#cumulative settlement by larval density, df 
Density_settlement <- settlement_raw_long %>% 
  group_by(Densities, DPF) %>% #initially group by DPF and density
  summarise(Settlers = sum(Settlers)) %>% #sum the settler count by day for each density
  arrange(Densities, DPF) %>% #arrange df by density and DPF
  group_by(Densities) %>% #cumulative sum only by density 
  mutate(Cum_sum = cumsum(Settlers)) #cumulative sum by density group over-time

#line plot!
ggplot(Density_settlement, aes(x = DPF, y = Cum_sum, colour = Densities)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)",y = "Approximate larval settlers", 
       colour = "Larval density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#68ebff", "#936bff", "#d4646c")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw()

#cum settlement by density normalized according to # of replicates in density groups
replicate_counts <- c("1 larvae/mL" = 8, #replicates in each density trtment
                      "2 larvae/mL" = 4, 
                      "5 larvae/mL" = 4, 
                      "15-20 larvae/mL" = 4)

#working df
Density_settlement_normalized <- settlement_raw_long %>%
  group_by(Densities, DPF) %>% #summarize by density and DPF
  summarise(Daily_settlers = sum(Settlers), .groups = "drop") %>% #sum by daily settlers
  arrange(Densities, DPF) %>% #rearrange df
  group_by(Densities) %>% #calculate and mutate by density groups
  mutate(Cum_sum = cumsum(Daily_settlers),
    Normalized_cum_sum = Cum_sum / replicate_counts[Densities]) #divide cumsum by # replicates

#plot
ggplot(Density_settlement_normalized, aes(x = DPF, y = Normalized_cum_sum, colour = Densities)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)", y = "Normalized cumulative daily settlement",
       colour = "Larval density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw()

#Surviving Juveniles in cohort overtime, df
test <- settlement_cohort %>% arrange(match(Date_interval, c("Month 3", "Month 6", "Month 9", "Month 12", "Month 15")))
```

```{r normalized CUMULATIVE settlement POSTER PLOT}
normalized_settlement_poster <- 
  ggplot(Density_settlement_normalized, 
         aes(x = DPF, y = Normalized_cum_sum, colour = Densities)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)", 
       y = "Normalized cumulative daily settlement",
       colour = "Larval Density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme(legend.position = 'top',
        panel.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'), #transparent panel bg
        plot.background = element_rect(fill='transparent', color=NA), #transparent plot bg
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), #remove major gridlines
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), #remove minor gridlines
        legend.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'), #transparent legend bg
        legend.box.background = element_rect(fill='transparent')) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = 'top')

ggsave('normalized_settlement.png', normalized_settlement_poster, bg='transparent')
```

```{r}
normalized_settlement_poster <- 
  ggplot(
    Density_settlement_normalized,
    aes(x = DPF, y = Normalized_cum_sum, colour = Densities)
  ) +
  geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  labs(
    x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)",
    y = "Normalized cumulative daily settlement",
    colour = "Larval Density"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme(
    # transparent backgrounds
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),

    # remove gridlines
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

    # white text everywhere
    text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "white"),

    # axes lines and ticks in white (important on dark backgrounds)
    axis.line = element_line(color = "white"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "white"),

    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "normalized_settlement_transparent.png",
  plot = normalized_settlement_poster,
  bg = "transparent",
  dpi = 300
)
```


```{r survivorship by DENSITY}
survivorship <- settlement_total

#density labels for plot
survivorship$Densities <- factor(
  survivorship$Density, 
  levels = c("1larva/ml", "2larva/ml", "5then2larva/ml", "Bucket"),
  labels = c("1 larvae/mL", "2 larvae/mL", "5 larvae/mL", "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#plot
ggplot(survivorship, aes(x = Densities, y = Survivorship, 
                         shape = Densities)) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = 'errorbar', width = 0.2) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Densities)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  theme_classic2() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```





## Statistical analysis -- settlement and survival (settlement data)
```{r DENSITY and SETTLEMENT}
##Did the pre-settlement density impact the number of settled stars?

Density_settlement_normalized_group <- settlement_raw_long %>%
  group_by(Densities, DPF, Group) %>% 
  summarise(Daily_settlers = sum(Settlers), 
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Densities, DPF) %>%
  group_by(Group) %>% 
  mutate(Cum_sum = cumsum(Daily_settlers),
    Normalized_cum_sum = Cum_sum / replicate_counts[Densities])

#df for tests
Normalized_sum <- Density_settlement_normalized_group %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DPF >= 146) %>%
  group_by(Densities) %>%
  mutate(se = sd(Normalized_cum_sum)/sqrt(length(Normalized_cum_sum)))

#mean for below graph
Normalized_mean_sum <- Density_settlement_normalized_group %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DPF >= 146) %>%
  group_by(Densities) %>%
  summarise(Mean_settle = mean(Normalized_cum_sum),
            se = sd(Normalized_cum_sum)/sqrt(length(Normalized_cum_sum)))

#visually check differences in mean
ggplot(Normalized_mean_sum, aes(x = Densities, y = Mean_settle, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_jitter(data = Normalized_sum, aes(y = Normalized_cum_sum, 
                                         colour = Densities, shape = Densities)) +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.1, colour = "red", 
                aes(ymin = Mean_settle - se, ymax = Mean_settle + se)) +
  labs(x = "Larval Density", y = "Mean normalized cumulative settlement") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177"))  +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())

by(Normalized_sum$Normalized_cum_sum, Normalized_sum$Densities, stat.desc)

leveneTest(Normalized_sum$Normalized_cum_sum, Normalized_sum$Densities, center = median) #appropriate, p = 0.88

ggplot(Normalized_sum, aes(sample = Normalized_cum_sum)) +
     geom_qq() +
     geom_qq_line() +
     facet_wrap(~Densities, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal()

shapiro.test(Normalized_sum$Normalized_cum_sum) 
#p = 0.623, overall normal, proceed with anova also given normality from NON normalized data

#ANOVA
Density_settlement_normal_aov <- aov(Normalized_cum_sum ~ Densities, data = Normalized_sum)
summary(Density_settlement_normal_aov) #p = 0.000431
plot(Density_settlement_normal_aov)

#robust anova
t1way(Normalized_cum_sum ~ Densities, data = Normalized_sum) #default 20%
##p = 0.01
t1way(Normalized_cum_sum ~ Densities, data = Normalized_sum, tr = .1) #trim only 10%
##p = 0.006
med1way(Normalized_cum_sum ~ Densities, data = Normalized_sum)
##p = 0.008

Density_settlement_normal_posthoc <- Density_settlement_normal_aov %>% tukey_hsd()
Density_settlement_normal_posthoc #view results

Density_settlement_normal_posthoc <- Density_settlement_normal_posthoc %>% arrange(p.adj)
Density_settlement_normal_posthoc <- add_xy_position(test = Density_settlement_normal_posthoc,
  data = Normalized_sum %>% as_tibble(.name_repair = "unique"),  #unique column names
  formula = Normalized_cum_sum ~ Densities, fun = "mean", x = "Densities")

x_levels <- levels(factor(Normalized_sum$Densities))

x_map <- setNames(seq_along(x_levels), x_levels)

Density_settlement_normal_posthoc <- Density_settlement_normal_posthoc %>%
  mutate(xmin = x_map[group1], xmax = x_map[group2])

Density_settlement_normal_posthoc <- Density_settlement_normal_posthoc
Density_settlement_normal_posthoc$y.position[1] <- 630 
Density_settlement_normal_posthoc$y.position[2] <- 570

#this plot took so long to trouble shoot.... genuinely... so long... its a MIRACLE it works
ggboxplot(Normalized_sum, x = "Densities", y = "Normalized_cum_sum", 
          color = "black", shape = "Densities") + 
  stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", width = 0.1) +
  geom_jitter(aes(x = Densities, y = Normalized_cum_sum, 
                  color = Densities, shape = Densities),
              data = Normalized_sum, width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(Density_settlement_normal_posthoc, 
                     label = "p.adj.signif", hide.ns = TRUE) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 680, label.x = "1 larvae/mL") +
  labs(x = "Larval density", y = "Normalized cumulative settlement") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177"))  +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 700)) +  #extend y-axis to fit signf brackets
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

report(Density_settlement_normal_aov)
```
A one-way ANOVA was performed to compare the effect of Density on cumulative larval settlement.A one-way ANOVA revealed that there was a statistically significant difference in cumulative settlement of larvae between at least two groups (F(3, 16) = 10.65, p = <0.01)). Tukey’s HSD Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of Length was statistically significantly different between the 1 larva/mL and the 5 and 15-20 groups.


```{r DENSITY and SURVIVORSHIP kruskal}
##Did different larval densities have different levels of survivorship to settlement?
##Given that: each group is in an 8L container and larval densities are approximate, not precise.

#density labels for plot
survivorship$Densities <- factor(
  survivorship$Density, 
  levels = c("1larva/ml", "2larva/ml", "5then2larva/ml", "Bucket"),
  labels = c("1 larvae/mL", "2 larvae/mL", "5 larvae/mL", "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#calculate survivorship as % rather than decimal
survivorship$Survivorship <- survivorship$Survivorship * 100

#descriptive stats
#by(survivorship$Survivorship, survivorship$Densities, stat.desc) 
##mean/sd:
#1 larva/ml = 13.11% / 8.4
#2 larva/ml = 6.3% / 2.9
#5 larva/ml = 3.4% / 1
#Bucket = 1.3% / 0.2

#variance test, levene test
#leveneTest(survivorship$Survivorship, survivorship$Densities, center = median) 
#non appropriate, p = 0.01

#checking normality within groups
ggplot(survivorship, aes(sample = Survivorship)) +
     geom_qq() +
     geom_qq_line() +
     facet_wrap(~Density, scales = "free_y")
shapiro.test(survivorship$Survivorship) #p = <0.01, non-normal

#shapiro.test by group
test <- survivorship %>% dplyr::select(Density, Survivorship) %>%
  filter(Density == "1larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Survivorship) #1 larva/ml p = 0.116

test <- survivorship %>% dplyr::select(Density, Survivorship) %>%
  filter(Density == "2larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Survivorship) #2 larva/ml p = 0.6345

test <- survivorship %>% dplyr::select(Density, Survivorship) %>%
  filter(Density == "5then2larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Survivorship) #5 then 2 larva/ml p = 0.035, NON NORMAL

test <- survivorship %>% dplyr::select(Density, Survivorship) %>%
  filter(Density == "Bucket")
shapiro.test(test$Survivorship) #bucket p = 0.611
##3/4 normal, do both KW and ANOVA to be robust

Settlement_survivorship_kruskal <- kruskal.test(Survivorship ~ Densities, data = survivorship)
Settlement_survivorship_kruskal #p = 0.002632

Settlement_survivorship_posthoc <- dunn_test(data = survivorship, Survivorship ~ Densities, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc

Settlement_survivorship_posthoc <- Settlement_survivorship_posthoc %>% arrange(p.adj)
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc <- Settlement_survivorship_posthoc %>% add_xy_position(x = "Density")

#super awesome plot
ggplot(survivorship, aes(x = Densities, y = Survivorship)) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = 'errorbar', width = 0.2) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(aes(shape = Densities, colour = Densities)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(Settlement_survivorship_posthoc, label = "p.adj.signif", 
                     hide.ns = TRUE, y.position = "y.position") +
  stat_compare_means(label.y = 31, label.x = "1 larva/mL") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177"))  +
  theme_classic2() +
  labs(y = "Larval settlement rate (%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank()) 

Settlement_survivorship_kruskal
```
A Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test was performed to compare the effect of larval density on percent survivorship through settlement and metamorphosis. The Kruskal-Wallis test revealed that there WAS a statistically significant difference in weight between at least two groups (KW Chi2(3) = 14.211, p = 0.002)). A non-parametric Dunn's Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of weight was significantly different between Bucket and 1 larva/mL (p = 0.002).There was no statistically significant difference between any of the other density treatments.


```{r DENSITY and SURVIVORSHIP aov}
Settlement_survivorship_aov <- aov(Survivorship ~ Densities, data = survivorship)
summary(Settlement_survivorship_aov) #p = 0.0145
plot(Settlement_survivorship_aov)

#robust anova
t1way(Survivorship ~ Density, data = survivorship) #default 20%
##p = 0.01022*
t1way(Survivorship ~ Density, data = survivorship, tr = .1) #trim only 10%
##p = 0.0052**
med1way(Survivorship ~ Density, data = survivorship)
##p = 0.008**

#post-hoc tests
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 <- Settlement_survivorship_aov %>% 
  tukey_hsd()
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 #view results

#work flow for publication-ready plot
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 <- Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 %>% arrange(p.adj)
Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 <- add_xy_position(test = Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2, data = survivorship %>% as_tibble(
  .name_repair = "unique"), formula = Survivorship ~ Densities, fun = "mean", x = "Densities")

x_levels <- levels(factor(survivorship$Densities))

x_map <- setNames(seq_along(x_levels), x_levels)

Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 <- Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 %>%
  mutate(xmin = x_map[group1], xmax = x_map[group2])

Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 <- Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2 %>% 
  mutate(y.position = y.position + 40)

Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2$y.position[1] <- 27 #specific y position of signf bracket

#anova results on plot
ggplot(survivorship, aes(x = Densities, y = Survivorship)) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = 'errorbar', width = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(colour = "black")) +
  geom_jitter(aes(shape = Densities, colour = Densities)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2, label = "p.adj.signif", 
                     hide.ns = TRUE) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 30) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0cb", "#e78ac3")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "% Larvae surviving to settlement") +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

report(Settlement_survivorship_aov)
```
A one-way ANOVA was performed to compare the effect of Density on cumulative larval settlement.A one-way ANOVA revealed that there was NOT a statistically significant difference in cumulative settlement of larvae between at least two groups (F(3, 16) = 2.97, p = 0.063)). Tukey’s HSD Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of Length was no statistically significantly difference between or within any of the density groups.

```{r DENSITY and SURVIVORSHIP POSTER PLOT}
density_survivorship_poster <- 
  ggboxplot(survivorship, x = "Densities", y = "Survivorship") +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Densities, shape = Densities), size = 2) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = 'errorbar', width = 0.2) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2, label = "p.adj.signif", 
                     hide.ns = TRUE, y.position = 27) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 30) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  labs(y = "Percent (%) survival to settlement") +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

density_survivorship_poster +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'), #transparent panel bg
    plot.background = element_rect(fill='transparent', color=NA), #transparent plot bg
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), #remove major gridlines
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), #remove minor gridlines
    legend.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'), #transparent legend bg
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill='transparent') #transparent legend panel
  )

ggsave('density_survivorship.png', density_survivorship_poster, bg='transparent')
```

```{r}
density_survivorship_poster <- 
  ggboxplot(
    survivorship,
    x = "Densities",
    y = "Survivorship",
    color = "white",   # ← white box outline
    fill  = NA         # ← transparent IQR
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(colour = Densities, shape = Densities),
    size = 2
  ) +
  stat_boxplot(
    geom = "errorbar",
    width = 0.2,
    colour = "white"
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    Settlement_survivorship_posthoc_2,
    label = "p.adj.signif",
    hide.ns = TRUE,
    y.position = 27,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  stat_compare_means(
    method = "anova",
    label.y = 30,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  labs(
    y = "Percent (%) survival to settlement",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),

    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

    text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "white"),

    legend.position = "none"
  )


ggsave(
  filename = "density_survivorship_transparent.png",
  plot = density_survivorship_poster,
  bg = "transparent",
  dpi = 300
)
```


```{r GAM of SETTLEMENT by DENSITY over DPF non normalized}
##Do daily settlement rates differ by density groups over time?

Settlement_dpf <- settlement_raw_long %>%
  mutate(Density = as.factor(Density),
         Group = as.factor(Group),
         Settlers = as.integer(Settlers), 
         DPF_scaled = scale(DPF)) 

#GAM 
Settler_gam_model <- gam(Settlers ~ s(DPF, by = Density) + Density, 
                 family = nb(), data = Settlement_dpf)
summary(Settler_gam_model)

#new df for settlement prediction across DPF range for each density group
new_data <- Settlement_dpf %>% group_by(Density) %>%
  reframe(DPF = seq(min(DPF), max(DPF), length.out = 100)) 

#adding the predicted fits on a response scale
new_data$Predicted <- predict(Settler_gam_model, newdata = new_data, type = "response")

#plot it
ggplot(Settlement_dpf, aes(x = DPF, y = Settlers, color = Density)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_line(data = new_data, aes(x = DPF, y = Predicted, color = Density), size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "GAM-Predicted Larval Settlement Trajectories by Density",
       y = "Daily Settler Count (Observed + Predicted)",
       x = "Days Post Fertilization") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

#plot GAM smooths apart from graphed data
plot(Settler_gam_model, pages = 1, se = TRUE, shade = TRUE)
```



```{r GAM of SETTLEMENT by DENSITY over DPF normalized}
##Do daily settlement rates differ by density groups over time? (with normalized data from density group replicates)

#Settlers by density normalized according to # of replicates in density groups
replicate_counts <- c("1 Larvae/mL" = 8, #replicates in each density treatment
                      "2 larvae/mL" = 4, 
                      "5 larvae/mL" = 4, 
                      "15-20 larvae/mL" = 4)

#working df
Settlement_dpf_normalized <- settlement_raw_long %>%
  group_by(Densities, DPF) %>% 
  arrange(Densities, DPF) %>% 
  group_by(Densities) %>% 
  mutate(Normalized_settlers = Settlers / replicate_counts[Densities], #div by # replicates
         Density = as.factor(Density),
         Group = as.factor(Group),
         Settlers = as.integer(Settlers))

#GAM 
Settlement_dpf_normalized$Densities <- as.factor(Settlement_dpf_normalized$Densities)
Settler_normalized_gam <- gam(Normalized_settlers ~ s(DPF, by = Densities) + Densities, 
                 family = nb(), data = Settlement_dpf_normalized)
summary(Settler_normalized_gam)
coef(Settler_normalized_gam)

#visualizing GAM outputs:
plot(Settler_normalized_gam, shade = TRUE) #4 splines output
abline(h = 0, lty = 'dashed') #adding line at zero

#plot predictions using marginal effects
#aggregated across density treatments
plot_predictions(Settler_normalized_gam, condition = 'DPF', type = 'link') +
  labs(y = "Linear predictor (link scale)", title = "Average effects of DPF", subtitle = "Aggregated across larval densities") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme_clean()

#by density treatment
plot_predictions(Settler_normalized_gam, condition = c('DPF', 'Densities'), type = 'link') +
  labs(y = "Linear predictor (link scale)", title = "Average effects of DPF", subtitle = "Aggregated by larval density") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  theme_clean()

#plotting GAM effect on response scale
#aggregated across larval densities
plot_predictions(Settler_normalized_gam, condition = 'DPF', type = 'response') +
  labs(y = "Expected response", title = "Average smooth effect of DPF on Settlement",
       subtitle = "Aggregated across all larval densities") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme_classic2()

#by density treatment (without obvs)
plot_predictions(Settler_normalized_gam, condition = c('DPF', 'Densities'), type = 'response') +
  labs(y = "Expected response", title = "Expected effect of DPF on settlement",
       subtitle = "Aggregated by larval density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#plot with regular observations on a smooth curve
ggplot(Settlement_dpf_normalized, aes(x = DPF, y = Normalized_settlers, 
                                      colour = Densities)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(fill = Densities), alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
   labs(y = "Observed response", title = "Observed effect of DPF on settlement",
       subtitle = "Aggregated by larval density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,40)) + 
  theme_bw()

#by density treatment WITH obvs barcoded
plot_predictions(Settler_normalized_gam, condition = c('DPF', 'Densities'), type = 'response', points = 0.5, rug = TRUE) +
  labs(y = "Expected response", title = "Average smooth effect of DPF on settlement",
       subtitle = "Aggregated within larval density treatments") +
  theme_bw()

#new df for settlement prediction across DPF range for each density group
Normalized_settlement_prediction <- Settlement_dpf_normalized %>% 
  group_by(Densities) %>%
  reframe(DPF = seq(min(DPF), max(DPF), length.out = 100)) 

#adding the predicted fits on a response scale
Normalized_settlement_prediction$Predicted <- predict(
  Settler_normalized_gam, newdata = Normalized_settlement_prediction, type = "response")

#plot it: GAM-predicted larval settlement trajectories by density
ggplot(Settlement_dpf_normalized, aes(x = DPF, y = Normalized_settlers, 
                                      color = Densities)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) + #make actual obvs more translucent
  geom_line(data = Normalized_settlement_prediction, #plotting GAM predictions
            aes(x = DPF, y = Predicted, color = Densities), size = 1) +
  labs(y = "Normalized daily settler count (observed + predicted)",
       x = "Days post fertilization") +
  theme_clean(base_size = 14) 

#plot GAM smooths apart from graphed data
plot(Settler_normalized_gam, pages = 1, se = TRUE, shade = TRUE)

#simulating from fitted GAM
#using linear predictor matrix Xlp
xp <- predict.gam(Settler_normalized_gam, type = 'lpmatrix')
dim(xp) #dimensions of prediction- 40 columns of basis functions for smooth terms in GAM
colnames(xp) #see smooth terms of spline
```



## Survivorship from settlement to juvenilehood
```{r plotting jitter and boxplot}
#Density labels for nicer plots
juvenile_settlement$Densities <- factor(
  juvenile_settlement$Density, 
  levels = c("1larva/ml", "2larva/ml", "5then2larva/ml", "Bucket"),
  labels = c("1 Larvae/mL", "2 larvae/mL", "5 larvae/mL", "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#calculative settlement rate (#juveniles in feb 25 / #settlers in july 24)
juvenile_settlement <- juvenile_settlement %>% group_by(Densities) %>%
  mutate(Survival_rate = Juvenile_total / Larval_total)

#mean, sd, summary statis for survival
Survival_summary <- juvenile_settlement %>% group_by(Densities) %>%
  summarise(mean_survival = mean(Survival_rate),
            sd = sd(Survival_rate),
            n = n())

#summary stats labels for graph
Survival_summary <- Survival_summary %>% mutate(label = paste0(
      round(mean_survival * 100, 2), "% ± ",
      round(sd * 100, 2), "%"))

#horizontal jitter with error bars
ggplot(data = juvenile_settlement, 
       aes(x = Densities, y = Survival_rate, color = Densities)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 2, alpha = 0.7, aes(shape = Densities)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar", width = 0.2, size = 0.7,
               color = "black", fatten = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(data = Survival_summary, 
                aes(x = Densities, ymin = mean_survival - sd, ymax = mean_survival + sd), 
                width = 0.2, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0cb", "#e78ac3")) +
  labs(y = "% Settlers surviving to 8 months post-settlement") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

#geom text with summary stats if you want to add them
#geom_text(data = Survival_summary, aes(x = Densities, y = mean_survival + sd + 0.01, label = label),inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.5, color = "black") +

#box plot, if that's more your flavor
ggplot(juvenile_settlement, aes(x = Densities, y = Survival_rate, fill = Densities)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_boxplot(geom ='errorbar', width = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(aes(shape = Densities)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0cb", "#e78ac3")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "% Settlers surviving to 8 months post-settlement") +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())
```


```{r jitter plot with point labels}
set.seed(123) #reproducibility
jitter_width <- 0.25 #spacing out jitters for label readability

#add numeric x-position and jitter to your data so jitter points are not random
juvenile_settlement <- juvenile_settlement %>%
  mutate(Density_num = as.numeric(Densities),
         jittered_x = Density_num + runif(n(), -jitter_width, jitter_width))

Survival_summary <- Survival_summary %>%
  mutate(Density_num = as.numeric(Densities))

#plot
ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data = juvenile_settlement,
             aes(x = jittered_x, y = Survival_rate, color = Densities, shape = Densities),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(data = juvenile_settlement, 
            aes(x = jittered_x, y = Survival_rate, label = Group), 
            size = 3, vjust = -1, show.legend = FALSE) +
 stat_summary(data = juvenile_settlement, aes(x = Density_num, y = Survival_rate),
               fun = mean, geom = "crossbar", width = 0.2, size = 0.7, 
              color = "black", fatten = 1) +
 geom_errorbar(data = Survival_summary, 
               aes(x = Density_num, ymin = mean_survival - sd, ymax = mean_survival + sd),
                width = 0.2, color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0cb", "#e78ac3")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:4, labels = levels(juvenile_settlement$Densities)) +
  labs(y = "% Settlers surviving to 8 months post-settlement") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

#reimport for cleaning purposes
juvenile_settlement <- read.csv("Juvenile settlment survival.csv")

ggplot(juvenile_settlement, aes(x = Settlement_survival, y = Juvenile_survival, colour = Density)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Group)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0cb", "#e78ac3")) +
  theme_classic2()

#plot and colour by diet? 
ggplot(juvenile_settlement, aes(x = Settlement_survival, y = Juvenile_survival, colour = Diet)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_classic2()
```


```{r linear regression JUVENILE and SETTLER SURVIVAL}
Settlement_survival_lm <- lm(formula = Juvenile_survival ~ Settlement_survival, data = juvenile_settlement)

Settlement_survival_lm
summary(Settlement_survival_lm)
sqrt(0.1136) #pearson coefficient is = 0.337046
AIC(Settlement_survival_lm) #AIC = -163.299

model.diag.metrics <- augment(Settlement_survival_lm)
head(model.diag.metrics)

#plotted residuals error (red) between observed values and fitted regression line
ggplot(model.diag.metrics, aes(Settlement_survival, Juvenile_survival)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm, se = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Settlement_survival, yend = .fitted), 
               color = "red", size = 0.3) +
  theme_classic2()
```


```{r pct SETTLEMENT over DPF}
##At what DPF, will x% of larva in x density treatment be settled? (predicted)

#prep data
Settlement_percentile <- Settlement_dpf_normalized %>% group_by(Group) %>% 
  group_by(Density) %>%
  arrange(DPF) %>%
  mutate(Cumulative_settlement = cumsum(Normalized_settlers),
         Percent_settled = Cumulative_settlement / max(Cumulative_settlement, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% ungroup()

#average percent settled across density treatments
Density_settlement_percentile <- Settlement_percentile %>% group_by(Density, DPF) %>%
  summarise(mean_percent_settled = mean(Percent_settled, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop')
  
#New gam with percentile data
Settler_percentile_gam <- gam(mean_percent_settled ~ s(DPF, by = Density) + Density,
                              family = nb(), data = Density_settlement_percentile)
summary(Settler_percentile_gam)
coef(Settler_percentile_gam)

#using gam to find the DPF at x% cumulative settlement
##generate predicted values from the GAM across DPF

#prediction grid, vary by 0.1 per day
pred_grid <- expand.grid(DPF = seq(min(settlement_raw_long$DPF),
                                   max(settlement_raw_long$DPF), by = 0.1),
                         Density = unique(Density_settlement_percentile$Density))

#predict values in prediction grid
pred_grid$Predicted_percent <- predict(Settler_percentile_gam, 
                                       newdata = pred_grid, type = "response")

#function, getting DPF at threshold
get_dpf_at_threshold <- function(df, threshold) {
  df %>% group_by(Density) %>%
    dplyr::filter(Predicted_percent >= threshold) %>%
    slice_min(DPF, n = 1) %>%
    mutate(Percentile = paste0(threshold * 100, "%")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Density, Percentile, DPF)
}

#retrieve the DPF for each percentile we want, i'm choosing 25%, 50%, 75%, and 90%
percentiles <- bind_rows(get_dpf_at_threshold(pred_grid, 0.25),
  get_dpf_at_threshold(pred_grid, 0.5),
  get_dpf_at_threshold(pred_grid, 0.9),
  get_dpf_at_threshold(pred_grid, 1.0))

print(percentiles) #retrieving DPF where density x will be at x%

percentiles$Percentile <- factor(percentiles$Percentile,
                                 levels = c("25%", "50%", "90%", "100%"))

#plot predictions with estimated DPF 
ggplot(pred_grid, aes(x = DPF, y = Predicted_percent, colour = Density)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_vline(data = percentiles, aes(xintercept = DPF, colour = Density), 
             linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ Percentile, nrow = 2, ncol = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#30d5c8", "#c0c0ff", "#f07177")) +
  labs(y = "Predicted Settlement (%)", x = "Days Post Fertilization (DPF)") +
  theme_bw()
```




```{r stat anal for all that stuff}
## Did the larval density impact the survival of new settlers to juveniles 8 months post-settlement? 
juvenile_settlement$Densities <- factor(
  juvenile_settlement$Density, 
  levels = c("1larva/ml", "2larva/ml", "5then2larva/ml", "Bucket"),
  labels = c("1 Larvae/mL", "2 larvae/mL", "5 larvae/mL", "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#mean, sd, summary stats for survival for graph below :)
Survival_summary <- juvenile_settlement %>% group_by(Densities) %>%
  summarise(mean_survival = mean(Juvenile_survival),
            sd = sd(Juvenile_survival),
            n = n())

#visually checking the differences in mean survival %
ggplot(Survival_summary, aes(x = Densities, y = mean_survival, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
   geom_errorbar(width = 0.1, colour = "red", 
                aes(ymin = mean_survival - sd, ymax = mean_survival + sd)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = "Larval Density", y = "Mean % survival to juvenile-hood") +
  theme_classic2()

#descriptive stats
by(juvenile_settlement$Juvenile_survival, juvenile_settlement$Densities, stat.desc)

#checking variances
leveneTest(juvenile_settlement$Juvenile_survival, juvenile_settlement$Densities, center = median) #appropriate, p = 0.2

#checking normality
ggplot(juvenile_settlement, aes(sample = Juvenile_survival)) +
     geom_qq() +
     geom_qq_line() +
     facet_wrap(~Densities, scales = "free_y")
shapiro.test(juvenile_settlement$Juvenile_survival) #p = <0.01, overall NON NORMAL

#shapiro.test by group
test <- juvenile_settlement %>% dplyr::select(Density, Juvenile_survival) %>%
  filter(Density == "1larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Juvenile_survival) #1 larva/ml p = 0.29

test <- juvenile_settlement %>% dplyr::select(Density, Juvenile_survival) %>%
  filter(Density == "2larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Juvenile_survival) #2 larva/ml p = 0.7873

test <- juvenile_settlement %>% dplyr::select(Density, Juvenile_survival) %>%
  filter(Density == "5then2larva/ml")
shapiro.test(test$Juvenile_survival) #5 then 2 larva/ml p = 0.5533

test <- juvenile_settlement %>% dplyr::select(Density, Juvenile_survival) %>%
  filter(Density == "Bucket")
shapiro.test(test$Juvenile_survival) #bucket p = 0.3371
##3/4 appropriate, between group non appropriate, proceeding with both KW and aNOVa to be robust

##KW
#kruskal-wallis 
Settlement_survival_kruskal <- kruskal.test(Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, data = juvenile_settlement)
Settlement_survival_kruskal #p = 0.747

#post-hoc tests
Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc <- dunn_test(data = juvenile_settlement, Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc
Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc %>% arrange(p.adj)
Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc %>% add_xy_position(x = "Density")



ggboxplot(juvenile_settlement, x = "Densities", y = "Juvenile_survival", color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Densities, shape = Densities), size = 2) + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(Settlement_survival_kw_posthoc, label = "p.adj.signif", 
                     hide.ns = TRUE) +
  stat_boxplot(geom ='errorbar', width = 0.2) +
  stat_compare_means(label.y = 0.021, label.x = "1 Larvae/mL") +
  labs(x = "Larval density", y = "Larvae surviving to 8-months post-settlement (%)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.021)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#68ebff", "#936bff", "#d4646c")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank()) 

#ANOVA
Settlement_survival_aov <- aov(Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, data = juvenile_settlement)
summary(Settlement_survival_aov) #p = 0.562
plot(Settlement_survival_aov)

#robust anova
t1way(Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, data = juvenile_settlement) #default 20%
##p = 0.0102
t1way(Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, data = juvenile_settlement, tr = .1) #trim only 10%
##p = 0.005
med1way(Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, data = juvenile_settlement)
##p = 0.008

#post-hoc tests
Settlement_survival_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_aov %>% tukey_hsd() #tukey's test
Settlement_survival_posthoc #view results, of course bucket and 1larva are signf....

#work flow for publication-ready plot
Settlement_survival_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_posthoc %>% arrange(p.adj) #arrange
Settlement_survival_posthoc <- add_xy_position(test = Settlement_survival_posthoc,
  data = juvenile_settlement %>% as_tibble(.name_repair = "unique"),  #unique column names
  formula = Juvenile_survival ~ Densities, fun = "mean", x = "Densities")

#factor levels in the order used in the plot
x_levels <- levels(factor(juvenile_settlement$Densities))

#vector to convert group names to x-axis positions
x_map <- setNames(seq_along(x_levels), x_levels)

#map to assign numeric values to xmin and xmax
Settlement_survival_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_posthoc %>%
  mutate(xmin = x_map[group1], xmax = x_map[group2])

#adjusting y.position to fit the signf brackets
Settlement_survival_posthoc <- Settlement_survival_posthoc %>% 
  mutate(y.position = y.position)
Settlement_survival_posthoc$y.position[1] <- 0.30 #specific y position of 2nd signf bracket

#this plot took so long to trouble shoot.... genuinely... so long... its a MIRACLE it works
ggboxplot(juvenile_settlement, x = "Densities", y = "Juvenile_survival", 
          color = "black") + 
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Densities, shape = Densities), size = 2) +
  stat_boxplot(geom ='errorbar', width = 0.2) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(Settlement_survival_posthoc, label = "p.adj.signif", hide.ns = TRUE) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 0.02, label.x = "1 Larvae/mL") +
  labs(x = "Larval density", y = "% Larvae surviving to settlement") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.02)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#92fe6a", "#68ebff", "#936bff", "#d4646c")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())

report(Settlement_survival_aov)
```
A one-way ANOVA was performed to compare the effect of Density on cumulative larval settlement.A one-way ANOVA revealed that there was NOT a statistically significant difference in cumulative settlement of larvae between at least two groups (F(3, 16) = 2.97, p = 0.063)). Tukey’s HSD Test for multiple comparisons found that the mean value of Length was no statistically significantly difference between or within any of the density groups. 


## Larval Growth -- from ImageJ photos
```{r larval growth}
#first pass visualization
plot(data = larval_growth, Size ~ DPF)

#ordering densities
larval_growth$Density <- 
  factor(larval_growth$Density, levels = c("1 larvae/mL",
                                           "2 larvae/mL",
                                           "5 larvae/mL",
                                           "15-20 larvae/mL"))

#working df
Larval_growth <- larval_growth %>% arrange(desc(Density)) %>%
  group_by(DPF, Density) %>%
  summarise(mean_size = mean(Size))

#first pass plot
ggplot(Larval_growth, aes(x = DPF, y = mean_size, colour = Density)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(y= "Average size (µm)", x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)") +
  theme_clean()

```

```{r larval growth POSTER PLOT}

Larval_growth <- Larval_growth %>% filter(!DPF == 42)

larval_growth_poster <- 
  ggplot(Larval_growth, aes(x = DPF, y = mean_size, colour = Density)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  labs(y= "Average size (µm)", x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)",
       colour = "Larval Density") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = 'top')

ggsave('larval_growth.png', larval_growth_poster, bg='transparent')
```

```{r}
Larval_growth <- Larval_growth %>% 
  filter(DPF != 42)

larval_growth_poster <- 
  ggplot(Larval_growth, aes(x = DPF, y = mean_size, colour = Density)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#006b3c", "#D2BAAD", "#9F8FAC", "#f3be77")) +
  labs(
    y = "Average size (µm)",
    x = "Days post fertilization (DPF)",
    colour = "Larval Density"
  ) +
  theme(
    # transparent backgrounds
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),

    # no gridlines
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

    # white text everywhere
    text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "white"),

    # white axes and ticks
    axis.line = element_line(color = "white"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "white"),

    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "larval_growth_transparent.png",
  plot = larval_growth_poster,
  bg = "transparent",
  dpi = 300
)
```
